decisions.summary: Block hard on PAN-1148 — consume VerbBadge, PhaseGlyph, IssueRow, MetricTile, MetricStrip primitives rather than reinventing them. If a primitive is missing when work starts, file a follow-up against PAN-1148 instead of duplicating it.

Flywheel is a new sixth lifecycle role (alongside plan/work/review/test/ship) but is NOT issue-scoped. It runs as a singleton Cloister-managed agent named `flywheel-orchestrator`. Role prompt lives at roles/flywheel.md; config under roles.flywheel in ~/.panopticon/config.yaml.

Enforce singleton via a Cloister gate row (`flywheel.globally_paused`) plus a SQLite-tracked `flywheel.active_run_id`. Spawn refuses if a run is already active; pause flips the gate and stops the tmux session without killing the run state.

Status delivery contract: orchestrator POSTs FlywheelStatus JSON to `POST /api/flywheel/status` on the local dashboard each tick. Dashboard validates against the contract, updates in-memory state, persists ONLY the latest snapshot to `~/.panopticon/flywheel/runs/<runId>/latest.json`, and broadcasts via Effect RPC stream `subscribeFlywheelStatus` over `/ws/rpc`. No per-tick SQLite churn.

Orchestrator-side helper command `pan flywheel emit-status --file <json>` wraps the POST so the role prompt never deals with raw HTTP. Schema-validates client-side before sending; bad payloads fail loud.

Default brief committed to `docs/flywheel-brief.md` (cleaned-up port of `~/Projects/ALL-UP-WITH-TWIST.md`). `pan flywheel start --brief <path>` overrides. No inline-in-config storage — briefs are markdown the team can diff and review.

Sidebar reshuffle (adding Pipeline, retiring Awaiting Merge) belongs to PAN-1148 and is NOT in scope here. This issue adds ONLY the Flywheel nav item under the existing Operations group, appended after Agents.

CLI surface: `pan flywheel start|pause|resume|status|report|emit-status`. Wrapper skill at `skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md` matching SKILLS-CONVENTION (lint-skills.sh enforces drift). Umbrella `pan` learns `flywheel` as a verb under the Operations bucket.

Conversation pane reuses the existing Claude Code conversation surface (the same one used for agent terminals/transcripts) scoped to the singleton `flywheel-orchestrator` session. No new transcript storage is introduced — the existing conversation/JSONL machinery already covers it.

Settings → Roles gains a Flywheel row mirroring the existing five role rows (harness, model, effort) plus two flywheel-specific knobs: maxAgents (concurrency cap) and scope (PAN-only | all-tracked-projects). Persisted under roles.flywheel in config.yaml; changes apply on the next tick without restart.

`pan flywheel report` writes `docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md` (replace) and appends a run-N section to `docs/OPERATION-FIX-ALL.md`, then creates a single `docs(flywheel): run N` commit. Toast in dashboard links to both files. Idempotent — running twice on the same run produces a no-op (or amend if uncommitted).

Auto-mode default for the orchestrator role config: harness=claude-code, model=opus-4.7, effort=high, maxAgents=8, scope=PAN-only. Mirrors tonight's proven run shape. Users edit via Settings → Roles → Flywheel.

hazards.summary: PAN-1148 primitives may not land before this issue's work agent starts. Work agent must verify VerbBadge/PhaseGlyph/IssueRow/MetricTile/MetricStrip exist as importable components before building the status pane.
hazards.mitigation: Block on PAN-1148 in plan edges. If pipeline tries to start work and PAN-1148 isn't merged, work agent must STOP and surface a blocker rather than reimplement the primitives.

hazards.summary: Orchestrator-spawned-by-orchestrator infinite loop. If the flywheel role gains the ability to start nested flywheel runs, recursion blows up Cloister.
hazards.mitigation: Cloister lifecycle bead (cloister-flywheel-lifecycle) MUST refuse to spawn a second flywheel-orchestrator while one is active. Add a unit test asserting the gate.

hazards.summary: Per-tick snapshot churn. A 5h run can emit hundreds of FlywheelStatus payloads. If stored in SQLite, it bloats the DB; if broadcast naïvely, it floods every connected client.
hazards.mitigation: Persist only `latest.json` to disk. Broadcast via Effect RPC stream, which already handles backpressure. End-of-run commits the report exactly once.

hazards.summary: Conversation pane and orchestrator role read from two different surfaces — the dashboard might show stale transcript while the tmux session is alive.
hazards.mitigation: Reuse the same conversation surface plumbing the agents page uses. Don't introduce a parallel transcript store.

hazards.summary: Brief discoverability. Operators may not know where briefs live; running `pan flywheel start` with no `--brief` should not surprise them.
hazards.mitigation: Default brief lives at `docs/flywheel-brief.md` and is read if no `--brief` is passed. Surface the resolved brief path in `pan flywheel status` and on the dashboard top-bar.

hazards.summary: Orchestrator host-vs-container assumption. The orchestrator needs host git/gh/tmux sockets; spawning it inside a workspace devcontainer breaks the single-Deacon invariant and the `~/.panopticon` host mount.
hazards.mitigation: Lifecycle bead MUST refuse to spawn flywheel-orchestrator when running inside a devcontainer (detect via PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON or hostname pattern). Document in roles/flywheel.md.

hazards.summary: Status contract drift between orchestrator emitter and dashboard reader. If the contract evolves and one side updates first, the dashboard crashes on bad payloads.
hazards.mitigation: FlywheelStatus contract lives in `@panctl/contracts` and is imported by BOTH the emit-status CLI helper (client-side validation) and the POST route (server-side validation). Schema is the single source of truth.

hazards.summary: Stop / pause semantics. Pausing a long-running orchestrator mid-tick may interrupt a spawn or merge it just initiated.
hazards.mitigation: Pause sets the gate so the orchestrator self-checks at tick boundaries and idles; it does NOT kill the tmux session. Resume clears the gate. Stop (kill) is a separate, explicit verb if ever needed.

hazards.summary: packages/contracts typecheck currently fails on pre-existing readonly schema output and ambiguous re-export errors in event-reducers/index; the FlywheelStatus build and package-local Vitest tests pass, but final root typecheck will require addressing these contract type errors.
hazards.mitigation: Before pan done, run npm run typecheck and fix the underlying contracts reducer/export typing errors rather than bypassing the gate.

resumePoint.description: Completed bead workspace-wjl7 (FlywheelStatus contract). Next agent should commit/close this bead if not already closed, then run bd ready -l pan-1189 and claim the next unblocked PAN-1189 bead.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1189: Bake the Fix-All Flywheel into Panopticon — sidebar tab, configurable orchestrator, standardized status, conversation pane
Planning complete: 12 auto-decisions recorded, 8 hazards identified, 23 beads decomposed across contract / role / lifecycle / server / CLI / UI / docs.
Implemented workspace-wjl7: added the FlywheelStatus Effect Schema contract, TypeScript interfaces, exports, package-local Vitest coverage, and verified packages/contracts build plus tests.
